site.name: AgenticOrg
site.description: AgenticOrg is an open-source platform for building, deploying, and governing enterprise AI agents with scoped tools, human approvals, audit evidence, and protocol-based integration.

pages.name: AgenticOrg
pages.title: Governed Enterprise AI Agents | AgenticOrg
pages.description: Build, deploy, and govern enterprise AI agents with scoped tools, human approvals, evaluations, audit evidence, native connectors, APIs, MCP, and A2A.
pages.summary: AgenticOrg is an open-source enterprise agent platform for designing workflows, connecting business systems, applying approval and policy controls, evaluating behavior, and retaining evidence of agent actions.
pages.primaryQuestion: How can enterprises deploy useful AI agents while retaining human control?

pages.name: Service Status
pages.title: AgenticOrg Service Status and API Health
pages.description: Check current AgenticOrg platform and API availability, release information, and links for investigating operational incidents or requesting support.
pages.summary: The status page reports the current availability information exposed by AgenticOrg and provides a support path when an organization needs incident-specific help.
pages.primaryQuestion: Is the AgenticOrg service currently available?

pages.name: Agent Evaluations
pages.title: AI Agent Evaluations and Quality Gates | AgenticOrg
pages.description: Review AgenticOrg evaluation methodology, quality dimensions, score interpretation, and the evidence teams should collect before promoting an agent.
pages.summary: AgenticOrg evaluations help teams assess quality, safety, reliability, security, latency, and cost before promoting an agent from testing into governed use.
pages.primaryQuestion: How should an enterprise evaluate an AI agent before production?

pages.name: Pricing
pages.title: AgenticOrg Pricing: Free, Pro, and Enterprise Plans
pages.description: Compare AgenticOrg Free, Pro, and Enterprise plans, including agent, run, storage, support, connector, SSO, SCIM, and service-level options.
pages.summary: AgenticOrg offers Free, Pro, and Enterprise tiers. Published limits and prices are generated from the same plan definitions used by the billing service.
pages.primaryQuestion: How much does AgenticOrg cost and what does each plan include?

pages.name: Agent Playground
pages.title: Enterprise AI Agent Playground | AgenticOrg
pages.description: Explore illustrative enterprise agent tasks, structured inputs, tool activity, confidence signals, and approval outcomes in the AgenticOrg playground.
pages.summary: The public playground demonstrates representative agent interactions with sample data. Its outputs are examples, not production decisions or performance guarantees.
pages.primaryQuestion: What does a governed enterprise agent run look like?

pages.title: Enterprise AI Agent Engineering Blog | AgenticOrg
pages.description: Read practical articles about governed AI agents, enterprise automation, evaluations, human approvals, OACP boundaries, integrations, and operations.
pages.summary: The AgenticOrg blog covers implementation patterns, operating practices, governance decisions, and protocol boundaries for enterprise AI agents.

pages.name: Resource Library
pages.title: Enterprise AI Agent Guides and Resources | AgenticOrg
pages.description: Browse AgenticOrg guides on agent architecture, automation use cases, governance, auditability, India-focused integrations, and OACP implementation.
pages.summary: The resource library provides educational guides for teams assessing, designing, integrating, governing, and operating enterprise AI agents.
pages.primaryQuestion: What should a team know before adopting enterprise AI agents?

pages.name: Integration Workflow
pages.title: AI Agent Integration Workflow and Controls | AgenticOrg
pages.description: Understand how AgenticOrg discovers connectors, scopes credentials and tools, validates access, tests workflows, requests approval, and records evidence.
pages.summary: The integration workflow takes a connection from discovery through scoped configuration, validation, shadow testing, approval, activation, and ongoing audit.
pages.primaryQuestion: How does AgenticOrg connect an agent to an enterprise system safely?

pages.name: Open Agentic Commerce Protocol
pages.title: Open Agentic Commerce Protocol (OACP) | AgenticOrg
pages.description: Learn the AgenticOrg OACP boundary for merchant source facts, Grantex authority, scoped artifact caching, buyer-safe answers, and provider-owned execution.
pages.summary: OACP separates commerce discovery evidence from execution authority. AgenticOrg can cache scoped artifacts and prepare a handoff, but cached evidence does not create an order, payment, mandate, inventory hold, refund, or paid state.
pages.primaryQuestion: What does OACP let an AI buying agent know and do?

pages.name: How Grantex Works
pages.title: How Grantex Delegated Authority Works | AgenticOrg
pages.description: Learn how Grantex authority artifacts, scopes, expiry, revocation, tenant boundaries, and audit evidence constrain delegated agent access in AgenticOrg.
pages.summary: Grantex represents delegated authority as bounded, inspectable artifacts. AgenticOrg validates the applicable scope and context before an authorized action or evidence-backed handoff.
pages.primaryQuestion: How does Grantex constrain delegated authority for an AI agent?

landingFaqs.question: What is AgenticOrg?
landingFaqs.answer: AgenticOrg is an open-source enterprise AI agent platform. It provides an agent runtime, workflow orchestration, native connectors, scoped tools, human approval gates, evaluation, audit evidence, SDKs, API access, MCP, A2A, and bounded OACP commerce surfaces.

landingFaqs.question: How does AgenticOrg keep AI agents under human control?
landingFaqs.answer: AgenticOrg combines role and tenant boundaries, scoped tool authorization, confidence and policy checks, shadow-mode evaluation, human-in-the-loop approval queues, version controls, kill switches, and audit records. Which controls apply depends on the configured workflow and deployment policy.

landingFaqs.question: How many agents, connectors, and tools are available?
landingFaqs.answer: The current counts are published by the live GET /api/v1/product-facts endpoint and rendered from the runtime registries. Public documentation links to that endpoint instead of maintaining a separate hard-coded count.

landingFaqs.question: What systems can AgenticOrg connect to?
landingFaqs.answer: The native registry covers finance, HR, marketing, operations, and communications systems. Optional integration gateways can extend that catalog. A listed connector is not proof that a tenant has configured credentials or approved write access; each action still requires the relevant scope, configuration, and provider availability.

landingFaqs.question: What is OACP in AgenticOrg?
landingFaqs.answer: The Open Agentic Commerce Protocol boundary separates merchant source facts, Grantex authority artifacts, AgenticOrg's scoped cache, buyer-safe answers, and provider-owned execution. Cached evidence can support discovery and a prepared handoff, but it does not create an order, mandate, payment, inventory hold, refund, or paid state.

landingFaqs.question: Can AgenticOrg be self-hosted?
landingFaqs.answer: Yes. The repository is Apache-2.0 licensed and includes Docker and deployment assets. The current managed production path uses Google Cloud Run with commit-pinned images, health verification, explicit traffic movement, and rollback-aware deployment tooling.

landingFaqs.question: Who is AgenticOrg for?
landingFaqs.answer: AgenticOrg is designed for engineering and operations teams that need governed agent workflows across finance, HR, marketing, operations, back office, communications, multi-company accounting, and buyer-seller commerce integrations.
